normalization.notes: Store Modern Hebrew text in NFC and preserve source strings for audit. Keep niqqud and cantillation marks available for analysis but do not add them to unpointed learner-visible words unless a lesson explicitly uses them as teaching aids.

segmentation.pedagogical_units: base consonant letters, five final-letter forms, matres lectionis, optional niqqud marks, and high-frequency consonant clusters
segmentation.notes: Modern Hebrew uses whitespace word boundaries but prefixes and suffixes can complicate morphology. Treat final forms as positional variants unless a lesson explicitly needs them as separate recognition targets.

sources.notes: Hebrew block metadata for letters, niqqud, cantillation marks, punctuation, and final forms. Use as infrastructure metadata only.

sources.notes: Hebrew exemplar characters and locale metadata. Confirm exact CLDR release before ingestion.

Short menu, sign, and label examples authored by GlyphBridge from reviewed vocabulary rather than copied from third-party corpora.
